---
title: "Creating A Bar Code Profile"
---

---
title: "Creating a Bar Code Profile"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/zwo1646248504051"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

Creating a new bar code profile is a two-step process. In the first step, you can define the bar code properties and in the second step, you can define how the bar code is to be used. You can navigate to the second page, the Usage Definition screen only after you have entered all the mandatory information in the first page, the Define Bar Code Profile screen. After you have successfully entered the relevant details, the step number beside the screen name changes to a green tick.

To create a bar code profile:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Bar Code Profiles tab. The Bar Code Profiles screen is displayed.    ![The Bar code Profiles list screen](data:image/gif;base64,)    Note: The bar code profile list displays the code type and specific value assigned to each bar code profile. It also provdes information on whether a bar code profile is used for separation on one or more content queues, or classification on one or more document classes.
2.  Click the + Create Bar Code Profile button. The Define Bar Code Profile screen is displayed.   ![Define Bar Code Profile screen](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  In the Define Bar Code Profile screen, enter a Bar Code Profile Name for the bar code profile. This is how the bar code profile is represented in the application.
4.  Enter a Bar Code Profile Description.
5.  Uploading a sample document containing a bar code helps to configure the bar code profile settings and ensures better bar code detec tion, automatically generating the bar code type, value, and scanning area. If a sample document is unavailable, you must manually configure these settings.
-
To upload a sample document with a bar code, drag or browse to a sample file and configure the associated options (see Uploading a Sample Bar Code Document).

- To manually configure the bar code type, value, and scanning area, proceed to step 6.
6.  Select the bar code type from the Bar Code Type drop-down list.  Tip:
See Supported Bar Code Types for a list of all supported bar code types.

When selecting a bar code type, also note the following:

-
You must specify a bar code type for all separation methods.

-
The Patch 2 and Patch T types are only respected if the Separate on every occurrence separation method is selected.

7.  Enter a value or multiple values in the Bar Code Value field, if applicable and press Enter. You can also use a comma to separate multiple values and press Enter.  Note: This field is only displayed if the Separate on specific value method is selected. This field is deactivated and displayed as an empty field if you select Patch 2 or Patch T from the Bar Code Type list. If the bar code profile was not saved with Patch 2 or Patch T bar code types, on selection of a different bar code type, the last saved bar code value is again displayed.   Note: If the profile is assigned for separation and Separate on specific value is selected as the separation method, the profile separates based on any value matching the values in this field.    Note: If the profile is assigned for classification, a document must contain one of the specific values for the type and region selected to classify the document. If no specific values are specified, the document is classified based on the type and region alone from the settings input on step 1 and ignore the actual value of the detected bar codes.
8.  To specify the bar code position manually, enter values in the following fields in the Bar Code Area and Bar Code Area Size sections:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Starting Position From Left | Specifies the starting position of the bar code area as measured from the upper-left corner of the page. This percentage value ranges from 0 to 99. |
| Starting Position From Top | Specifies the starting position of the bar code area as measured from the top of the page. This percentage value ranges from 0 to 99. |
| Width | Specifies the width of the bar code area with respect to the page. This percentage value ranges from 1 to 100. |
| Height | Specifies the height of the bar code area with respect to the page. This percentage value ranges from 1 to 100. |

9.  Click the Next button. The Usage Definition screen is displayed.   ![Usage Definition screen](data:image/gif;base64,)    Note: The Next button is activated only when all the relevant information has been filled in all the mandatory fields.
10.  In the Usage Configuration screen, in the Separation Options section, from the Select Separation Method drop-down list select one of the following options:

-

-

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Separate on every occurrence | Separates the document every time a specific type of bar code is detected. This is the default separation method. |
| Separate on change | Separates the document when the bar code value changes. This method does not consider a specific bar code value; it separates the document only when a change in bar code values is detected. When selecting the Separate on change method, also note the following:   The first detected bar code of the specified type is considered a change and triggers a split, even when no prior value has been detected.   Since only one value can be used for processing, the Separate on change method does not separate the document if it detects multiple bar codes that match the acceptance criteria. |
| Separate on specific value | Separates the document when a bar code of a specific value is detected. When this method is selected, the Bar Code Value field is displayed on the Create Separation Profile screen. Enter the specific bar code value in the field. |

11.  Select one of the following options in the Select page for separation section:
| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Bar code identifies the first page of the document | The bar code used for separation identifies the first page of the document. |
| Bar code identifies the last page of the document | The bar code used for separation identifies the last page of the document. |

12.  To delete the page containing the bar code that is used for document separation, select the Delete the page which is containing the barcode used to separate a document check box.
13.  The Assigned Content Queues list, displays all the content queues assigned to this bar code profile.
14.  To delete the page containing the bar code that is used for document classification, in the Classification Options section, select the Delete the page which is containing the barcode used to classify a document check box.
15.  The Assigned Classes list, displays all the classes assigned to this bar code profile.
16.  Click Save.  Note: To go to the Define Bar Code Profile screen, click the Back button.
